reasoning: Although the US conducted major airstrikes on Iranian nuclear facilities in late June 2025 (Operation Midnight Hammer) and sent extra troops to the Middle East for defense and support, a territorial invasion remains highly unlikely. The resolution criteria specifically require a military offensive "intended to establish control over any portion of Iran." Both President Trump and Vice President JD Vance have indicated there is no intention of deploying ground troops to Iran. An invasion to establish control over territory would require massive logistical preparation and troop mobilization, which is not currently underway as of November 2025. With less than two months remaining in the year, and clear signals that the US approach is focused on airstrikes rather than a ground war aiming for territorial conquest, the probability of an invasion in 2025 is near zero. Allowed probabilities bottom out at 5%, so I will predict 0.05.
